title: Crispy-Caramelized Flaky Tunisian Kielbasa Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic stew originating from Tunisia, featuring caramelized onions and crumbly kielbasa.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large copper pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are dark golden brown and caramelized (about 30 minutes).
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the kielbasa and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides (about 10 minutes).
Add the tomato paste, ground cumin, and smoked paprika. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Add the beef broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro (optional).
To make crispy onions, heat 1-2 tbsp olive oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 1-2 cups sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy and golden brown (about 10-15 minutes).
Sprinkle the crispy onions over the tagine before serving.
